local_dishes.name: Kefli Becha
local_dishes.description: The chicken is cooked in wine
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Djiz-biz
local_dishes.description: A lamb/sheep meat dish that is fried
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Bozbash
local_dishes.description: Traditional Azerbaijani soup, containing lamb, red/white beans, green vegetables, herbs
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Dushbara
local_dishes.description: Tiny dough, filled with meat and local herbs
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Lavangi
local_dishes.description: This dish typically is prepared with a whole fish, seasoned with local herbs and spices, the whole dish is then baked
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Buglama
local_dishes.description: Buglama in Azeri means 'steamed', a traditional lamb stew that is slow-cooked for approximately two hours
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Piti
local_dishes.description: This supreme Azeri soup is famous for its authenticity in the regions of Azerbaijan. The broth is made of lamb and chickpeas
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Dolma
local_dishes.description: A traditional Azerbaijani dish, stuffed with meat, and vegetables blended with traditional Azerbaijani spices and then wrapped in grape leaves
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Shah Plov
local_dishes.description: This Azerbaijan dish consists of a rice base, topped with meat, and dressed with a crust fried dough
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Saj platter
local_dishes.description: Something of a cross between a tawa and kadhai, a saj platter transforms meat and vegetables into a delectable family meal! Chicken wings, lamb or even fish is laid out in the centre of the saj and slices of delicious fried eggplant, tomato, potato, peppers and chilli are arranged around it
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Levengi
local_dishes.description: A paste made of fried—almost caramelised—onions, walnuts, and dried fruits. It is traditionally used as a stuffing for chicken or fish. Sometimes qutabs (flatbreads) are made with levengi stuffing too
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Qutab
local_dishes.description: Stuffed with minced meat, greens and cheese
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Magal Salaty
local_dishes.description: A traditional and famous salad with a combination of seasonal vegetables, herbs. Make sure to ask for without meat
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Coban Salatasi
local_dishes.description: Called a shepherds salad, made with a combination of chopped tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, green peppers, lemon juice, olive oil, and parsley
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Dovg
local_dishes.description: Dovga is a soup made by cooking yogurt with a variety of fresh herbs such as dill, mint, and coriander. It is the national dish of Azerbaijan and can be served either cold in the summer as a refreshment, or warm in the winter
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Serdakh
local_dishes.description: The dish is usually made with a combination of eggplants, butter, garlic, tomatoes, vegetable stock, salt, and pepper. The garlic is sautéed in butter, then removed from the pan
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Baklava
source_files: Azerbaijan and Georgia/Rimmi_Azerbaijan Final Itinerary.pdf
Azerbaijan and Georgia/Azerbaijan- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Azercell, Bakcell and Nar Mobile are the sim card service providers in Azerbaijan. Azercell is considered to be the best in terms of coverage.

You can buy a physical sim or e-sim by visiting an Azercell retail store or authorized reseller and show your ID. The most recommended pack is for 10 AZN which gives you 3 GB of data and 300 minutes of domestic calling.

You can activate international roaming packs on your India number. Even if you choose not to purchase an international roaming pack, do ensure your roaming is activated to receive SMS such as for OTPs.

health_tips.tip: Travel Insurance is not mandatory but it is recommended. Tata AIG offers a good package.
Carry doctors prescriptions/travel prescriptions for medicines (except over the counter meds).
